section.name: secret-prevention
section.summary: .gitignore patterns for secret files, pre-commit hook for blocking secret patterns in staged code, and audit commands.

rule.statement: Add .gitignore patterns for .env files at the root of every repository before the first commit; retroactively removing committed secrets requires rewriting history.
rule.rationale: A secret committed once is in the git history even after deletion. History rewriting is destructive and requires coordinated force-push across all clones. Prevention is the only safe path.

rule.statement: Install a pre-commit hook that scans staged file content for API key patterns; a .gitignore miss still blocks the secret at the commit boundary.
rule.rationale: Defense in depth: .gitignore prevents accidental addition, pre-commit hooks catch secrets in code files (hardcoded tokens, test fixtures). Both layers are needed.

section.name: gitignore-patterns

example.code:
# Environment files .env .env.local .env.production .env.staging .env.*.local *.env
# Keep examples and templates !.env.example !.env.tpl
# Cryptographic keys *.pem *.key *.p12 *.pfx id_rsa id_ed25519 id_ecdsa
# 1Password session .op/
# Credential files credentials.json service-account.json token.json

section.reference: .gitignore block covering all common secret file patterns

section.name: pre-commit-hook
section.reference: pre-commit hook blocking Anthropic/OpenAI key patterns and PRIVATE KEY in staged files

example.code:
# Install the hook in a repository cp templates/pre-commit-secrets.sh .git/hooks/pre-commit chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
# Test: stage a file with a fake key echo 'API_KEY=sk-ant-api03-fake' > test.py git add test.py git commit -m "test" # Should be blocked
# Clean up git rm test.py

# Check if any .env files are tracked in git git ls-files | grep -E '\.env$'
# Search git history for known secret patterns (requires git-secrets or truffleHog) git log --all --full-diff -p | grep -E 'sk-ant-api|PRIVATE KEY' | head -20
# Check file permissions on all .env files find ~/ -name "*.env" -not -path "*/node_modules/*" -exec stat -c "%a %n" {} \;
# Find world-readable files in /srv that might be secrets find /srv -maxdepth 4 -name ".env" -perm /o+r 2>/dev/null
